dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7477 IH: Fair Markets and Sports Integrity Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Titus introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Commodity Exchange Act to prohibit entities registered under such Act from engaging in transactions involving sporting event or casino-style gaming contracts.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fair Markets and Sports Integrity Act.

section.header: Prohibitions relating to sporting event and gaming contracts
section.text: Section 4c of the Commodity Exchange Act (7 U.S.C. 6c) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Sporting event and gaming contract prohibition

paragraph.text: A registered entity may not list for trading, facilitate, or clear any agreement, contract, or transaction that is based on, references, or derives its value from, or otherwise involves—

subparagraph.text: any sporting event or athletic competition; or

The term casino-style game means any game customarily offered in casinos or gambling establishments, including slot machines, blackjack, roulette, craps, poker, bingo, lotteries, or any digital or simulated version of such games.

The term sporting event or athletic competition means any live, simulated, or virtual event involving physical or mental skill or athletic performance in which 1 or more participants or teams compete or face a challenge and an outcome, score, or statistical measure is determined, including amateur, intercollegiate, or professional sports.
